methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
ANYbotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $150M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private robotics firms at similar stage, supplemented by one public comparable.

Two methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $150M total raised proxy, yielding $285M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables (1X Technologies at $10.0B, Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B, Dexterity at $1.6B, Collaborative Robotics at $500M, and Fairmat at $350M) produces a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects genuine heterogeneity in robotics subsector positioning and commercialization maturity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $242M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median point of $680M. Stage-progression anchors the lower bound conservatively; peer median establishes the upper bound. Key uncertainties include ANYbotics' specific product-market traction relative to the peer set, the sustainability of valuations at the $1.0B+ level given sector-wide commercialization timelines, and whether the company's legged robotics focus commands a premium or discount to the broader mobile manipulation cohort represented in comps.
